race.name: Ski to the Sun
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through the Methow Valley's 200-kilometer trail network — North America's Nordic paradise, finishing at Sun Mountain Lodge.

vitals.aid_stations: 3-4 stations with water, sports drink, snacks

climate.description: The Methow Valley sits in the rain shadow of the Cascades, receiving reliable dry snowfall. Race day temperatures typically range from -10°C to 0°C. Blue skies are common, but Arctic outflow events can bring extreme cold.
challenges: Possible Arctic outflow creating bitter cold and wind
Afternoon sun softening trails at lower elevations
Variable wax conditions across elevation changes
Remote valley location complicates logistics if weather turns

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Methow Valley trail system, traversing forest, meadow, and river valley terrain. Finishes with a climb up to Sun Mountain Lodge at 3,000 feet with panoramic views of the North Cascades.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails on North America's largest cross-country ski trail network
course.grooming: Excellent grooming standard, both classic and skate lanes maintained by Methow Trails
features: Point-to-point finish at Sun Mountain Lodge with panoramic views
North America's largest groomed trail network (200+ km)
Final climb to Sun Mountain Lodge is a signature challenge

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: World-class grooming on North America's largest trail network. Young race with small but passionate field. Remote location limits accessibility but rewards with stunning North Cascades scenery.

history.summary: Founded in 2019, Ski to the Sun is a relatively new race that leverages the Methow Valley's extraordinary trail infrastructure. The Methow Trails system, maintained by a dedicated nonprofit, is the largest groomed cross-country ski trail network in North America with over 200 kilometers of trails. The race quickly gained a cult following, selling out annually as word spread about the spectacular point-to-point course finishing at the iconic Sun Mountain Lodge.
notable_facts: The Methow Valley has over 200 km of groomed trails — the largest network in North America
Sells out every year despite the race's youth
Sun Mountain Lodge finish provides one of the most scenic race endings in Nordic skiing
The Methow Trails nonprofit maintains the entire network through community support
The valley sits in the Cascade rain shadow, creating reliable cold, dry snow
